title: berlinia auriculata
intro:
Berlinia auriculata is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Sapotaceae, and is commonly known as the 'ear-shaped Berlinia' due to its distinctive ear-shaped leaves.

The plant has been traditionally used in various African cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits. Berlinia auriculata is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in water to alleviate symptoms, and its leaves can also be used to make a poultice or decoction for topical application.
